reasoning: Q1 fails correctness validation due to 2 critical issues: (1) hardcoded dictionary mapping swaps returnflag codes 1↔2, routing rows to wrong GROUP BY groups (semantic error, MUST fix), and (2) delta decoding has O(n²) complexity from re-decoding entire prefix on each morsel. Both are high-impact and low-risk. Dictionary fix restores correctness; delta decode fix adds 5-10% performance. Proceeding with optimization is essential.
focus_areas: Fix dictionary mapping for returnflag column - parse lineitem_dicts.txt correctly instead of hardcoding {'A', 'N', 'R'} as {'A', 'R', 'N'}
Optimize delta decoding from O(n²) to O(n) by storing last decoded value between morsels
Validate GROUP BY correctness after fixes - ensure rows map to correct (returnflag, linestatus) groups
strategy_notes: Priority 1: CRITICAL - Fix dictionary parsing. Lines 188-189 hardcode rf_dict = {'A', 'N', 'R'} but actual order from lineitem_dicts.txt is A(code 0), R(code 1), N(code 2). Replace with: (1) parse lineitem_dicts.txt line-by-line, (2) extract code→value mapping, (3) use during aggregation (lines 295-300) and output (lines 379-380). Expected outcome: 100% correctness restoration. Priority 2: Optimize delta decoding (lines 269-283). Current re-decodes from index 0 for each morsel; change to incremental: store last_decoded_value from previous morsel, use as base for next morsel. Reduces O(n²) to O(n), saves ~5-10% wall-clock time. Both fixes have clear implementation paths and low regression risk.
notes: These are CORRECTNESS-critical and PERFORMANCE-critical fixes. Dictionary fix must complete before performance work is meaningful. After fixes, expect Q1 to match GenDB's 48ms baseline and produce correct results. Current 98.98ms execution time is masked by 100% wrong output on 50% of rows.
